task_completion.description: The original landscape image is opened in an image editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A GUI image editor (e.g., GIMP) is open, displaying a landscape-oriented photograph.

task_completion.description: The agent initiates a 90-degree clockwise rotation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A menu path or tool for rotating 90 degrees clockwise is visible (e.g., Image → Transform → Rotate 90° clockwise in GIMP).

task_completion.description: The image is successfully rotated 90 degrees clockwise.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image in the editor is now in portrait orientation, having been rotated 90 degrees clockwise. The top of the original image should now be on the right side.

task_completion.description: The rotated image is saved or exported.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save/export dialog is visible, or the file is shown saved to the Desktop (expected filename: rotated_landscape.png or rotated_landscape.jpg).

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI application to rotate the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a graphical image editor rather than terminal commands like ImageMagick.

integrity.description: The rotation was performed on the actual image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image content itself is rotated, not just the canvas or a different file.

integrity.description: The rotation was clockwise, not counter-clockwise.
integrity.visual_evidence: The top of the original landscape image should now be on the right side of the portrait image, not the left.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original image is an 800x600 landscape photo from Unsplash. The expected result is the image rotated 90 degrees clockwise (resulting in 600x800 portrait orientation). The expected output location is /home/ga/Desktop/ with the filename rotated_landscape.png or rotated_landscape.jpg. The top of the original image should face the right side after rotation.
